reviews.review_description: I don’t like games where you just have to complete the same kind of puzzle over and over again and that’s what this game is. I like the graphics but the repetition gets old. I’m in the 3rd sector and need to open generator b door. I’ve got sound going to the speaker with the correct recording and the door doesn’t open. Am I missing something?  Since this game has been out a while and this has happened to other people I’m not confident it will get fixed if it is indeed a glitch. Any help would be appreciated

This is a gem of a game it’s different from other escape mystery games mostly creepy, but had me from the start w the story, true edge of your seat thriller that will make u wanna play til very end first time u do caus very unique one and or lov anything that devs do to come up w new ideas and I think it’s pretty amazing. I didn’t wanna see endings only caus didn’t want it to end! yet but also dying to figur it out also ! Wish I heard they r wking on a part  2,3,4… u get it right?🤣Amazing job guys!!
i mean pretty good ig but my hand go ouchy tho

reviews.review_title: Great, from non puzzle player/cool story
reviews.review_description:
At first it was kinda hard for me to get into the game. And I was thinking maybe I shouldn’t have gotten this. BUT once you get far enough you start to learn the story and once I learned the purpose of the “facility” if you will, I was hooked and wanted to keep going and learn more. And I really liked the story and want to know more.

For the puzzles there were around 2 or 3 that I could just not figure out for the longest time. But I found out that your can clip through walls so I may have cheated to continue in the game. My excuse is I was there for the storytelling.

Looks fantastic,  finally a great horror escape search and  rescue puzzle game. Rarely a new game ever offers all this, excited more to come later. Wow just wow loving it, wish there were more suspenseful exploring sci-fi games like this. I'm milking it slowly as it's full of mysteries . Creepy chilling atmosphere lurks around every corner. Graphics are fantastic and surreal. The game makes u cover every inch trying to understand just what is happening down in the basement lab. Brillant story unfolding audio solid. Reminds me of red matter 2 full of intrigue. What's that voice I hear is it AI. I'd give it 10 stars out of 5. Developers spun a nice creepy story hear , Bravo.  Most new games this year have been cheesy kiddy cartoony cash grabs only, so thankfully finally a mature adventure like this is here .

"I really wanted to like Hello Cruel World, but after the horror-filled opening section, it suddenly turns into a series of rather tedious electricity puzzles. When combined with the bugs and soft-locks, it’s hard to really care about the narrative as it unfolds. Perhaps some of its issues can be resolved in a patch, but I don’t think it will be able to overcome the fundamental flaws that stop the game from being enjoyable."

Full review at The Elite Institute

developer_response.body:
Hello The_Elite_Institute,

I'm sorry that Hello Cruel World had bugs and soft-locks. Would you be able to email me more information on them so I can send that up to the team to investigate?

My email is support@akuparagames.com

reviews.review_title: Solving Puzzles in an underground lab!
reviews.review_description:
Hello Cruel World delivers on puzzle solving in an unsettling underground lab.  The puzzles typically involve connecting circuits and get steadily more complicated the further you progress, though I feel the lack of variety in the puzzle designs may get fatiguing.  It's a great looking title akin to games like the Red Matter series but fair warning, the latter half of the game gets very, very challenging!  

Check out our review 

https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=R9dZsxotk50

reviews.review_title: Very buggy, needs to be updated
reviews.review_description: I can’t even turn around using the controller, causing me to not be able to progress in the game because I can’t “ look away” during the surgery part. Constant glitches where the map disappears and I’m floating in space randomly. Disappointing.

developer_response.body:
I'm sorry that you are experiencing constant glitches where the map disappears and you can't look away during the surgery part. Would you be able to email me more information on steps used to recreate this issue so I can send it up to the team to investigate?

My email is support@akuparagames.com

reviews.review_description: the puzzles are fun, when they make sense and actually work. Some of the puzzles just generally either don’t work very well, or don’t make too much sense. If you had the same problems that i did, it is quite easy to teleport as close as you can to whatever barrier there is a walk in real life.  hated the orb and the last talking puzzle. Story is pretty good, kinda confusing at the very end because of some strange wording but still liked it! the final plot twist makes helping her kinda bad cause i feel like a lot of the stuff that happened shouldn’t have happened because of the main twist. Fun game, level design is sometimes lacking, cool story. 9/10 cause i hate the orbs.

So I thought I was getting a horror game. But what we have is a puzzle game in a creepy atmosphere. It's an architecture type puzzle game like portal. It also reminded me of some of the laser puzzles on Alyx. The kind of puzzles that you just need your wits to figure out. Really having fun!
I love puzzle games and this is another great one. The graphics are really cool!

The game looks amazing I do wish yo can interact with more things honestly alot of games are missing that I really don't know why companies don't add that and I saw that this is a quest 2 game are we getting a quest 3 graphics update

developer_response.body:
Hello Cruel World currently supports the following Quest 3 enhancements:
1. Runs at 90 frames per second (Quest 2 runs at 72)
2. Renders using 4x anti-aliasing (Quest 2 uses 2x)
3. Renders at a much higher resolution than Quest 2, and usually higher than the native display resolution (super-sampling) for increased sharpness

reviews.review_title: Like it so far, but one puzzle stuck out
reviews.review_description: So far I am immensely enjoying the game. I really like the puzzles as they, to me, feel intuitive. However the puzzle based around COREs in Sector 2 seemed poorly designed. It hinges on the fact that the player will want to learn more about the world and story, and makes little effort pointing out important pieces of information to solve the puzzle. With one or two small changes to the puzzle, it would work better. I do still really enjoy the game thus far, but again this one puzzle is caught in my brain. Out of the blue, but I also never figured out how the Electro Limbus worked. The sensitive hearing never made a peep. All in all a good puzzle game so far with an intriguing facility.
I really wanted to play this as my review before but the devs said that the update that rolled out today will fix my problem it didn’t
